sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a 34-year-old male who has been experiencing neck pain since a car crash in February of this year.
He has had multiple car crashes, with the most recent one occurring when he was coming home from a pain clinic.
He reports severe pain, rating it a 12 out of 10, which is sharp and incapacitating.
He has been unable to work due to the pain.
He has a history of previous car crashes, including one in which he had surgery, and has been to therapy for several months.
He is currently taking no medications, but has been prescribed fentanyl in the past.
He denies any fever, but reports fatigue since the accident.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of multiple car crashes, including one requiring surgery.
He has been to therapy for several months and has a history of neck pain.
Review of systems: The patient reports neck pain, headaches, dizziness, numbness in the left arm and right leg, and spasms in his body.
Current medications: None
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has pain on palpation of the neck, both on the bony process and muscle.
He has a moderate range of movement in his neck, but reports pain when bending forward and backward.
He has no bruising or swelling in the neck area.
He reports fatigue.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, which can be explained by the multiple car crashes he has been in.
Problem list: Neck sprain; Active.
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500mg every 6-8 hours to help relax the muscles in the area and alleviate pain.
I also advised the patient to apply ice to the affected area and to perform exercises to improve range of motion.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy and will consider local injections if necessary.
I will also refer the patient to pain medicine if the Robaxin is not effective.
I will order an MRI to further evaluate the extent of the injury.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the MRI results are available, and I will re-evaluate his treatment plan at that time.
I will also give the patient time off work until the MRI results are available.
